q: What does MCP stand for, and what is it?
choices: Multi-Channel Plugin — a way to broadcast prompts to many apps at once
Model Context Protocol — a standard that lets an AI agent talk to one outside app via a small server
Master Control Program — the central scheduler inside Hermes
Managed Claude Pipeline — a wrapper that batches Claude API calls
explain: MCP is the open standard. A single MCP server wraps one app's API and exposes a list of tools (find_issues, create_issue, etc.) the agent can call.

q: As of June 2026, which apps ship in the built-in Hermes MCP catalog?
choices: Linear, n8n, and Unreal Engine
Linear, GitHub, and Notion
GitHub, Discord, and Telegram
Only Linear
explain: Linear, n8n, and Unreal Engine. Run `hermes mcp catalog` after every `hermes update` — the list will grow.

q: You want to connect Hermes to GitHub. The catalog doesn't have it. What should you do?
choices: Give up — without the catalog, GitHub integration isn't safe.
Find a community GitHub MCP, read its source, and configure it yourself.
Run `hermes mcp install github` anyway and see what happens.
Wait for the catalog and don't use GitHub in the meantime.
explain: Custom MCPs are the right path for apps the catalog doesn't cover — but only after you've read the source and understand what the MCP is doing.

q: What's the most important defensive habit after installing any MCP?
choices: Run `hermes update` immediately after install.
Review the tool whitelist during install, and re-review it with `hermes mcp configure <name>` later.
Set up a separate API key for every team member.
Disable the MCP at night so it can't run while you sleep.
explain: The whitelist is the most important defensive control. Review it at install; re-review with `hermes mcp configure` later. Don't accept defaults blindly.

q: Short answer: name the four commands that make up the catalog workflow.
explain: `hermes update`, `hermes mcp`, `hermes mcp install <name>`, `hermes mcp configure <name>`.

q: After uninstalling an MCP, what should you do with the cached credentials?
choices: Nothing — Hermes cleans up automatically.
Manually delete the token from `~/.hermes/mcp-tokens/` (or the API key from `~/.hermes/.env`).
Re-install the MCP to overwrite the old token.
Run `hermes update` to flush the cache.
explain: Uninstalling an MCP doesn't delete the cached OAuth token or API key. Clean up `~/.hermes/mcp-tokens/` and `~/.hermes/.env` for any MCP you've removed.
